title: SVC = common cardinal veins (chest CT)
vignette: Septic patient gets a central line into a structure embryonically derived from the common cardinal veins. On axial chest CT, which labeled structure (A=SVC)?

clues.text: central line into a structure from the common cardinal veins
clues.note: Central lines terminate in the SVC; SVC derives from the common cardinal veins.

whatMatters: Central line tip = SVC; SVC = adult derivative of the (right) common cardinal vein. Pure CT-ID task.
strategy: On axial CT, patient's right = image left. SVC sits anterior + to the patient's right of the ascending aorta.

eliminations.correctIf: Question asked about an aortic arch anomaly (vascular ring, persistent right aortic arch)
Question asked about truncus arteriosus defect or pulmonary trunk development
Question asked about a structure derived from foregut endoderm (esophagus, stomach)
Question asked about a posterior mediastinal vessel derived from the paired dorsal aortae

reasoning: Central line tip terminates in the SVC
SVC = right common + right anterior cardinal veins
On CT, SVC is anterior + patient's right of ascending aorta
mechanism: Central venous catheters advance until the tip is in the SVC. The SVC forms from the right common cardinal and right anterior cardinal veins (cardinal system also -> brachiocephalic, azygos).

keywords.back: Right common + right anterior cardinal veins

memoryHook: Central line ends in the SVC; the SVC comes from the cardinal veins.
